title: Fetch->snapshot->harmonize->trend+CI pipeline (one indicator)
context: M1 requires a reproducible end-to-end pipeline that fetches published aggregate screening-coverage data, commits immutable checksummed snapshots, harmonizes to the canonical schema, and computes trend plus confidence/credible intervals from source standard errors. The pipeline must enforce aggregate-only access (no micro-data paths), 100% provenance coverage, and full reproducibility from cached snapshots in CI. This builds on the canonical schema (004) and proven gate (005). The first indicator is breast/mammography, US national and states, from accepted PD sources.
objective: Implement the end-to-end fetch -> snapshot -> harmonize -> trend + CI pipeline for one indicator (breast/mammography, US national + states) from accepted public-domain sources. The pipeline must write immutable checksummed snapshots, harmonize to the canonical schema, compute trends and confidence/credible intervals from source standard errors, enforce aggregate-only access, and regenerate all outputs byte-stably from cached snapshots in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline fetches breast/mammography screening-coverage aggregates from accepted US PD sources (BRFSS and/or NHIS) using published aggregate APIs or table exports only; any code path that could reach micro-data or individual-level records is rejected at runtime

Every fetch writes an immutable, checksummed (SHA-256) source snapshot with provenance metadata (source URL, publisher, retrieval timestamp, dataset version, license id + URL, Wayback save URL); analysis runs against snapshots, not live sources
Harmonization maps each series to the canonical trend-series schema (task 004) with all required fields populated and harmonizationScore >= 90 per series

Trend computation is transparent and documented: trend over time, change vs. baseline, confidence/credible intervals propagated from source standard errors; no smoothing or modeling beyond what is documented and reproducible; model-based source estimates (e.g., PLACES) carry their model caveats intact
CI regenerates all dataset outputs byte-stably from committed snapshots with no live-source network access; provenance-coverage gate (100%) and license-status gate both pass
Aggregate-only access protocol enforced in code: any attempt to fetch individual-level or controlled-access data raises an error
Statistical reviewer sign-off recorded before merge
output: A merged PR implementing the fetch -> snapshot -> harmonize -> trend + CI pipeline for breast/mammography (US national + states) from accepted PD sources; CI green with byte-stable reproducibility from snapshots, 100% provenance coverage, and license-status gate passing; statistical reviewer sign-off recorded.
